posts.author: Michał Rukszan
posts.title: My Brother's American Adventure (and How I Survived Without Him)
posts.excerpt: While my brother was busy living the American dream—from soccer in Arkansas to Spring Break in Mexico—I was back home in Poland navigating the bittersweet reality of 8th-grade stress, tricky time zones for gaming, and the glorious (yet lonely) luxury of having two bedrooms all to myself.
posts.body:
My older brother went on a high school exchange to the USA when he was 17. He lived in Arkansas, attended an American high school, and even played for the school soccer team. During his exchange, he traveled a lot – he visited Los Angeles, Florida, Las Vegas, and went on a cruise to Mexico for Spring Break!

To be honest, when he left, I didn’t care that much. I didn’t even say a proper goodbye. Just as I expected, the time flew by really fast, but there were moments when it slowed down and I really missed him. I am used to being alone, but this time I felt truly lonely. Knowing he was just a phone call away wasn't the same.

I missed his support the most when I was in the 8th grade. I was extremely stressed about my final exams. My parents were there for me, but my brother had already passed these exams and could have given me the best advice. Somehow, I managed to do it all without him and I survived!

We sometimes called each other to play video games together. However, it wasn't easy because of the 7-hour time difference. When it was 7:00 PM here in Poland, it was only noon in Arkansas. Since he had his own school schedule, we could only play on weekends or holidays.

But there was one huge advantage to his absence! I had both of our rooms entirely to myself. I used one room as my gaming and studying area, and the other one just for sleeping and keeping my things. It honestly made my life so much easier during that time!



\[Photo 2]

This is the amazing view from his Spring Break cruise. Just look at this beautiful beach in Mexico!

\[Photo 3]

And this is him in Las Vegas, standing in front of the famous Sphere with his host mom. The best part is that she is coming to visit us in Warsaw this summer!

![](/images/highschool.jpg "Here is a picture of my brother with his friends from his favorite AP History class. You can see his school building in the background.")
